title: Mobility Metrics Platform
description: Full stack platform for real-time mobility monitoring — Flutter mobile app and NestJS web admin panel.
longDescription: Designed and delivered a complete mobility analytics solution for Vortex Soluciones SAS: a Flutter mobile app for field data collection and a NestJS-powered web admin for real-time monitoring, visualization, and reporting of mobility metrics across multiple locations.
problem: Operations teams had no centralized, real-time visibility into mobility data. Information was fragmented across manual processes with no tooling to collect, process, or visualize metrics reliably.
solution: Built a scalable NestJS backend exposing REST APIs documented with Swagger, a Flutter mobile app published to Google Play, a web admin dashboard with real-time analytics views, and role-based access control with JWT authentication.
architecture: Layered NestJS backend with modular services, REST APIs documented with Swagger, PostgreSQL with Sequelize ORM, JWT-based auth with RBAC, Docker containerization, Railway cloud deployment, and Flutter client consuming the same API.
challenges: Processing and centralizing diverse mobility data sources in real time; designing a mobile-first experience for field workers while keeping the web admin panel responsive and secure; managing deployment across cloud infrastructure and Google Play Store.
results: Delivered a production-ready platform with mobile app published on Google Play, real-time dashboards for operations teams, and a scalable cloud infrastructure ready for additional data sources and user growth.

title: Virtual World Cup Album
description: Gamified digital collectibles platform inspired by football sticker albums, with social mechanics, referral system, and rewards.
longDescription: A personal project building a gamified collectibles experience for the FIFA World Cup: users collect virtual stickers, invite friends through a referral system, and unlock rewards. Built with Flutter for mobile and NestJS for the backend, following clean architecture principles for future expansion.
problem: Traditional sticker albums are physical, single-use, and inaccessible to remote participants. The opportunity was to recreate the excitement digitally with added social layers, reward mechanics, and real-time engagement.
solution: Designed a Flutter mobile client backed by a NestJS API with Prisma on PostgreSQL. Implemented a referral and invitation system with JWT-secured authentication, Maps API integration for location features, and clean architecture to support future trading and social features.
architecture: Clean architecture NestJS backend with Prisma ORM on PostgreSQL, JWT authentication, Maps API integration, Docker containerization, and Flutter mobile client with state management and offline-ready patterns.
challenges: Designing a referral reward system that scales without gaming exploits; implementing near-real-time collection updates without overloading the backend; structuring the data model to support future sticker trading and social interactions.
results: Active personal project with core collection flow, JWT auth, referral system, and Maps integration operational. Architected for future expansion including sticker trading, social feeds, and leaderboards.

title: Community Marketplace
description: Hyperlocal marketplace with geolocation, nearby store discovery, and an order management system with workflow states.
longDescription: A personal project for a community-focused marketplace where users discover nearby stores on a map, browse products, and place orders with full workflow tracking. Designed with scalability and security in mind from the ground up.
problem: Local businesses lacked a digital channel to connect with nearby customers, and buyers had no easy way to discover and order from neighborhood stores without relying on large, impersonal platforms.
solution: Built a NestJS backend with TypeScript, Prisma, and PostgreSQL implementing geolocation-based store discovery, an order management system with workflow state transitions, secure JWT authentication, and a multi-role permission model.
architecture: NestJS modular backend with Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL for geospatial and transactional data, JWT-based auth with role separation (buyer, seller, admin), Docker containerization, and geolocation query support for nearby store discovery.
challenges: Implementing accurate geolocation queries for nearby stores with reasonable performance; designing a flexible order state machine adaptable to different business types; securing multi-role access without over-complicating the permission model.
results: Fully operational backend with geolocation discovery, order state workflows, and multi-role JWT auth — ready for frontend integration and user testing with a well-documented API surface.

title: ATEC Learning Management System
description: Full-featured LMS for course management, content delivery, enrollment flows, and learning progress tracking.
longDescription: Designed and built a Learning Management System for ATEC enabling institutions to manage virtual courses, instructors, and student journeys. Includes content distribution, enrollment workflows, progress tracking, and administrative modules with role-based access.
problem: The institution managed virtual learning through fragmented manual processes with no centralized platform for course creation, student enrollment, content distribution, or progress reporting.
solution: Built a NestJS backend with TypeScript and PostgreSQL implementing course and content management, user enrollment flows, learning progress tracking, admin modules for instructors and students, and clean architecture with secure JWT auth.
architecture: Modular NestJS backend following clean architecture principles, Sequelize ORM on PostgreSQL, Swagger-documented REST APIs, role-based access control separating student, instructor, and admin domains, Docker deployment on Railway.
challenges: Designing a flexible content structure for different course formats and media types; implementing a permissions system that cleanly separates student, instructor, and admin roles without tight coupling; ensuring the data model supports future feature expansion.
results: Complete LMS backend supporting course lifecycle, enrollment, content delivery, and progress tracking — deployed on Railway with a fully documented API and role-based access control.

title: Wheels Gogo
description: Carpooling app reducing commuting costs and carbon footprint by connecting drivers and passengers for shared rides.
longDescription: A full stack personal project developed over nearly two years: a carpooling platform where drivers and passengers coordinate shared trips to reduce individual transport costs and vehicle emissions. The mobile app was published on Google Play and served real users.
problem: Urban commuters faced high individual transport costs and environmental impact with no easy, trusted way to coordinate shared rides among peers — especially in mid-size cities with limited public transit.
solution: Built a full stack solution: a Flutter mobile app published on Google Play, a Node.js/Express REST API backend, MongoDB for flexible data storage, and Azure for cloud infrastructure — covering the full journey from trip creation to passenger matching.
architecture: Flutter mobile client published on Google Play, Node.js/Express REST API, MongoDB for document storage and flexible trip data modeling, Azure cloud infrastructure for backend deployment and scaling.
challenges: Coordinating real-time trip matching between drivers and passengers with low latency; building trust and safety mechanisms into the matching flow; managing the full app publishing lifecycle on Google Play.
results: Published on Google Play and active from 2023 to 2025 with a complete carpooling flow — trip creation, driver-passenger matching, and ride management — serving real users in the target city.
